provenance.purpose: v1 gap-closure: behaviorally isolate the market / population / replacement sub-gates of position_is_as_needed_squad_appointable (src/squads.cpp + src/fort_admin.cpp).
provenance.companionSourcePin: tools/harness/b293_noble_squad_availability_test.mjs asserts the C++ predicate contains the HAS_BEEN_REPLACED / HAS_MET_POP_REQ / REQUIRES_MARKET / HAS_MET_MARKET_REQ clauses; this fixture proves each clause is behaviorally load-bearing.
provenance.dfStructures: df.entity.xml entity_position flags: HAS_BEEN_REPLACED, requires_population, REQUIRES_MARKET

note: possibleAppointable is EMPTY on purpose: with nothing DF-offered, the AS_NEEDED squad fallback is the ONLY path to creatable, so each excluded variant is excluded SOLELY by the one sub-gate it violates. Every variant below is clean on ALL OTHER gates (number:-1 AS_NEEDED, squadSize>0, a non-empty appointedBy whose office is held).
